data.description:
## Summary
The `verify-identity` job added to `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` in commit 5febe8274
("run verify-identity as a PRE-MERGE whole-store gate") more than DOUBLED gerrit-verify CI
runtime: runs jumped from ~12-27 min (through sha cb0f8ec3b) to 44-72 min, first spiking to
43.8 min on 5febe8274 itself. The `verify-identity` job is the sole long pole (66.2 min in a
66.6-min run); every other job is unchanged.

## Root cause (profiled against the real store: 2572 events in scope, 808 signed)
`rebar verify-identity` (the step) = 63.5 min; the git fetch is only 2.2 min. Phase timing:
- `_collect_all` (whole-store walk): 1.9s
- `build_introducing_commit_map` (batched git log, bug 1cc0): 2.3s
- classify UNSIGNED events: ~0ms each (short-circuits)
- classify SIGNED events: 1,780 ms EACH -> ~24 min local / ~63 min on slower CI runners

cProfile of one signed classify: ~100% of time in `subprocess.run`, via
`_verify_signed -> verify_authorship_at_commit -> keys_valid_at_anchor -> resolve_event_commit`
= a full-history `git log --diff-filter=A` PER keyring record, PER signed event (5 git spawns
/event). This is the exact O(events × keyring × history) cost bug 1cc0's
`build_introducing_commit_map` was built to kill, but the batched map was only wired into the
enforcement/grandfather decision (`_resolve_commit`), NOT the era-verify hot path — verified
events `continue` before reaching it.

Also: `_classify` runs UNCONDITIONALLY; `identity.require_authenticated` only gates the final
pass/fail verdict, never whether the per-event git work runs. So enforcement being off does
not avoid the cost.

## Config reality
require_authenticated = true (enforcement ON). enforce_since pinned to tracker HEAD at re-flip
time, so only events introduced AFTER it are enforced; all earlier events are grandfathered
(reported, never fail). The gate git-verifies all 808 signed events but only post-enforce_since
events can change the verdict.

## Fix plan (3 parts)
1. Gate the expensive per-event era-verify when NOT enforcing (require_authenticated off):
   classify signed events cheaply (signed vs unsigned) in advisory mode. VERIFIED: the era
   result feeds only (a) the exit code when enforcement is ON and (b) informational counts /
   --format json (consumed only by unit tests). Both CI workflows gate on exit code only,
   which is unconditionally 0 in advisory mode. Safe.
2. Skip ARCHIVED tickets in `_collect_all` (reuse the reduce already done in
   `_is_gate_exempt_ticket`; status == "archived"). Free and authoritative.
3. Wire `--base`/grandfather-boundary scoping into the gate so collection is bounded to
   `enforce_since..HEAD` (via existing `_collect_range`), eliminating the git-heavy
   re-verification of provably-grandfathered history. Behavior-preserving for the enforced
   verdict.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Advisory mode (require_authenticated=false) does NOT run per-event git era-verification;
      exit code stays 0; whole-store scan drops to seconds.
- [ ] Archived tickets are excluded from the scan (with a regression test).
- [ ] With enforcement ON, collection is scoped so only enforce_since..HEAD events are verified,
      AND the security invariant holds: an unsigned/forged event introduced AFTER enforce_since
      still FAILS the gate (held-out test), while pre-boundary events are not git-verified.
- [ ] gerrit-verify `verify-identity` job wall-time returns to the low-single-digit minutes.
- [ ] Full suite + lint + typecheck green; landed via Gerrit (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).
data.title: verify-identity whole-store gate: 63-min CI runtime (O(events×keyring×history) git subprocesses)
